title: Ayurveda Profile
description: Ayurvedic interpretation of an HerbalPreparation. Uses Ayurvedic concepts like Rasa (taste), Guna (qualities), Virya (potency), Vipaka (post-digestive effect), and Dosha effects. This profile INTERPRETS a preparation through the Ayurvedic theoretical framework.

profiles.description: The HerbalPreparation that this profile interprets.

hasRasa.description: Rasa (रस) - The six tastes according to Ayurveda. Each taste has specific effects on the doshas and body. Most dravyas have one primary rasa, some have multiple.

rasas.madhura: Sweet (मधुर) - Increases Kapha, decreases Vata/Pitta, nourishes tissues
rasas.amla: Sour (अम्ल) - Increases Pitta/Kapha, decreases Vata, stimulates digestion
rasas.lavana: Salty (लवण) - Increases Pitta/Kapha, decreases Vata, softens tissues
rasas.katu: Pungent (कटु) - Increases Vata/Pitta, decreases Kapha, stimulates digestion
rasas.tikta: Bitter (तिक्त) - Increases Vata, decreases Pitta/Kapha, cleanses tissues
rasas.kashaya: Astringent (कषाय) - Increases Vata, decreases Pitta/Kapha, binds tissues

hasGuna.description: Guna (गुण) - The qualities or attributes of the dravya. There are 20 gunas (10 pairs of opposites). A dravya typically has several gunas.

hasVirya.description: Virya (वीर्य) - The potency or energetic effect. There are two main viryas: Ushna (heating) and Sheeta (cooling). This is crucial for understanding how the dravya affects the doshas.

effects.ushna: Heating - Increases Pitta, decreases Vata/Kapha, stimulates metabolism
effects.sheeta: Cooling - Decreases Pitta, can increase Vata/Kapha, pacifies heat

hasVipaka.description: Vipaka (विपाक) - The post-digestive effect. After digestion, the rasa transforms into one of three vipakas which have long-term effects on the body.

effects.madhura: Sweet vipaka - Nourishes tissues, increases Kapha
effects.amla: Sour vipaka - Increases Pitta, can aggravate blood disorders
effects.katu: Pungent vipaka - Increases Vata, reduces Kapha

hasPrabhava.description: Prabhava (प्रभाव) - The special or unique effect that cannot be explained by rasa, virya, or vipaka. This represents idiosyncratic or specific actions.

affectsDosha.description: How this dravya affects the three doshas (Vata, Pitta, Kapha). Each dosha can be increased (vriddhi), decreased (kshaya), or balanced (sama).

ayurvedaCategory.description: Ayurvedic classification of the dravya (e.g., Rasayana, Medhya, etc.).

karma.description: Karma (कर्म) - The actions or therapeutic effects in Ayurvedic terms.

ayurvedaModernResearch.description: Modern scientific research relevant to Ayurvedic use.

classicalReferences.description: References to classical Ayurvedic texts (Charaka Samhita, Sushruta Samhita, etc.).

anupana.description: Anupana (अनुपान) - The vehicle or carrier substance used to administer the dravya (e.g., milk, honey, warm water).

sevanaKala.description: Sevana Kala (सेवन काल) - The optimal time to take the dravya.

_documentation.keyDifferenceFromTCM: Ayurveda uses Rasa-Guna-Virya-Vipaka-Dosha framework, not Nature-Flavor-Meridian like TCM
_documentation.keyDifferenceFromWestern: Ayurveda uses energetic properties (virya, guna) rather than pharmacological actions
